preamble: The alveolar trill [r] shifts to the voiced retroflex lateral approximant [ɭ] through retroflex harmony and place assimilation in South Asian languages. This transformation is documented in Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, and Malayalam as both a historical sound change and a contemporary allophonic variation where r adopts retroflex articulation in retroflex-conditioned phonological environments.

examples.note: Allophonic shift in retroflex harmony contexts; historically distinct phonemes now merge in some dialects
Intervocalic and retroflex-conditioned environments trigger lateralization
Casual/rapid speech allophone in retroflex contexts
Contemporary variation in retroflex harmony environments; historically robust three-way distinction (l, ḷ, r) now shows merger patterns

related.label: Alveolar trill to lateral approximant (similar process without retroflexion)
